enriched_points.expanded_content: High-speed rail systems face engineering constraints such as track alignment and material fatigue, impacting performance and operational efficiency, as highlighted in risk assessment studies.
Accurate data acquisition for HSR dynamics is hindered by sensor limitations and environmental factors, complicating the analysis of performance metrics and safety evaluations.
Safety risks in high-speed rail operations include collision prevention, emergency response protocols, and compliance with regulations, necessitating ongoing research and improved safety frameworks.
